data.narrative:
Proxima Fusion is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised €145M to date. The valuation universe for nuclear frontier-tech comprises 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power. This narrow comp set reflects the early-stage, capital-intensive nature of advanced nuclear development.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The nuclear energy sector lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables at similar valuation tiers—only 1 same-stage and 1 adjacent-stage peer were identified against a requirement of 3+ datapoints for statistical relevance. A broad sector median would obscure material differences in technology maturity, funding runway, and commercialization timelines across the comp universe.

Without a triangulated result from multiple methods, this analysis cannot isolate a defensible valuation range. Material uncertainties remain around Proxima's tokamak development timeline, regulatory pathways, and capital requirements to commercial demonstration. Valuation work should proceed via scenario modeling against specific technical milestones and dilution paths through Series B-C, informed by individual comp analysis rather than statistical medians. The extreme heterogeneity of this sector—spanning fusion approaches, fission modalities, and fuel cycles—argues against formulaic approaches and instead requires method-by-method assessment tied to Proxima's distinct positioning.
